purpose: Defines the `!project` command for managing the target project path. Three modes: `!project` (display current project, resolution method, CLAUDE.md presence, .synapse/ presence, tech stack indicators), `!project set /path/to/repo` (validate path, write {tracker_root}/.synapse/project.json with current_project + set_at, create {project_root}/.synapse/), `!project clear` (remove current_project from project.json, revert to CWD detection). Establishes the canonical resolution order: explicit --project flag > stored config in .synapse/project.json > CWD.
gotchas: {project_root} is ALWAYS an absolute path — relative paths to !project set must be resolved
Resolution order is strict: --project flag > stored config > CWD (third tier is the agent's CWD, not the user's shell)
Validation only WARNS (doesn't block) when: no .git/ directory, no project markers (package.json/CLAUDE.md/src/), empty/home directory
task.project_root is stored in initialization.json when a swarm starts — different dashboards can serve different projects
Projects should add .synapse/ to their .gitignore — this is a setup recommendation
patterns: Three sub-commands documented separately under one parent command
Stored config schema: { current_project: string, set_at: ISO8601 }
Display output enumerates: resolved path, resolution method, CLAUDE.md status, .synapse/ status, tech stack indicators (package.json/tsconfig.json/Cargo.toml/go.mod/requirements.txt)
conventions: Different layout from other Synapse commands — no Steps section; uses 'How It Works' subsections per mode
Trailing Notes section for cross-cutting concerns
